ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-02-2008 Continued To Jul-16-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan for and with Doris Butler (Executor). Katherine Allen (Daughter). Carol Allen (Daughter). Gail Collins (Grandaughter).

Mr. Cowan stated that the 13100 declarations had been filed except for Kathy's and Carol's. The Parties were directed to meet in hall way and try to resolve the issues. Upon returning into the Courtroom, Mr. Cowan stated that he had the consent to act as Trustee and the two remaining 13100 declarations. The family members stated that they were upset about not having received some of the personal property from the estate. The Court advised all parties to seek counsel so that they could present there concerns properly before the Court. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aguino.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-28-2008 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Receipt For Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jan-02-2008 Continued To Feb-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Robert Cowan appearing for and with Doris Butler, executor. Mr. Cowan stated that he will need to order the bank statements from the bank to file the final account and petition for final distribution. He requested for a 4 weeks continuance. The Court questioned him on the filing of the blocked account receipt. Mr. Cowan stated that he has filed the blocked account receipt. The Court did not see any entry in the ROA and instructed counsel to bring back a filed endorsed copy this morning. Other family members were in court and they objected to the continuance. The Court asked Mr. Cowan to stay in the courtroom and this case will be called in its order.
Case was called at 9:23am. Robert Cowan appearing for and with Doris Butler, executor. Ruby Allen and Carol Allen were in court. Ruby Allen complained about Mr. Cowan not filing the accounting and the family members not getting any information from him except for a copy of the final accounting in the conservatorship. Carol Allen complained about Mr. Cowan not responding to her request to view the contents of the house. Mr. Cowan stated he was informed by the bank that it will take 3 to 5 days to get the bank statements and he will need one week to do the accounting. He also stated that he has set a date for the family to view the contents of the house but he did not get response before that date. The Court advise both Ms. Allens to get legal advise, that it may be too late to challenge the conservator's final account if it was approved by the court and that they will need to file a petition to bring their issues before the court. The matter was continued to 2/4 for status on filing of final account and petition for final distribution. The Court directed Mr. Cowan to send a copy of the accounting with the notice of hearing to all parties. He is to return to court before the end of the day with a copy of the blocked account receipt.

Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Receipt For Blocked Account
Off Calendar Dec-11-2007 Continued To Jan-02-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES- ATTY. ROBERT COWAN FOR THE PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE DORIS BUTLER;
CAROL ALLEN (HEIR) ALSO APPEARED IN COURT.

ATTY. COWAN REPORTED TO THE COURT THAT HE JUST RECEIVED A NOTICE YESTERDAY THAT THE FUNDS WERE DEPOSITED IN THE BLOCKED ACCOUNT BUT HE HAS NOT RECEIVED COPIES OF THE RECEIPT. HE REQUESTED THE CONTINUANCE FOR THE FILING OF THE RECEIPT AND FINAL ACCOUNT. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO JANUARY 2, 2008. THE COURT DIRECTED MS. ALLEN TO RAISE HER ISSUE AT THE NEXT HEARING.

JUDGE: JOHN DEARMAN; REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN, CSR #11722 CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT
BAILIFF: FELICIA SMITH

Off Calendar Jul-23-2007 Continued To Aug-27-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Robert Cowan appearing for and with Doris Butler, executor. Carol Allen is also in court. Counsel reported that the executor has been working on clearing the property. She is almost done. The For Sale sign is up. It will be ready for sale tomorrow. The listing agreement will start on 8/1. Carol Allem questioned the withdraw of $15,000.00 for repair work. Counsel reported that the request for withdraw of funds for repair work was denied. The Court has allow withdraw from the blocked account to pay Golden Gate Disposal for rental of the dumpsters. Court stated that there will be an accounting for estate funds. Carol Allen requested to go to the house and look for some things with sentimental value. The executor reported that she did not find any pictures. Court wanted counsel to set a time to allow Ms. Allen to go to the property. Ms. Allen questioned the executor paying her son and his girlfriend to do the clean up. Court has not authorized payment to anyone for helping. Counsel reported that the executor has written a check to pay Sunset Scavenger. Court is concerned about the bank not blocking the account. Court ordered the executor not to write any more check from the account. She can file a petition for payment. The listing broker Mr. Rock in in court. He has nothing to add. Matter is continued to 8/27 for status of sale. Mr. Cowan and Ms. Butler are ordered to appear unless Report of sale and petition for confirmation of sale is filed. OSC is continued to 8/27.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: R. Drocco.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Hearing On Exparte Petition For Release Of Funds
Off Calendar Apr-12-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan (Counsel for the Petitioner, Doris Butler).

The Court noted that when the matter was continued from Monday (April 9, 2007) the Proof of Mailing ("POM") needed to be filed. Mr. Cowan noted that POM had been filed. The Court also noted that Mr. Cowan needed to submit an Amended Petition and the Order to Approve Release of Funds. Mr. Cowan noted that the original Petition had been filed and returned to him as an Endorsed Copy by mistake. The Court gave Mr. Cowan permission to refile the original Petition as an Amended Petition. The Court granted the Petition for Withdrawal of Funds from Blocked Account (signing the Order for same in open court). After Mr. Cowan questioned the powers of the Executor with regard to the listing agreement, the Court noted that the Executor has limited independent powers and that she may move forward with the listing agreement.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Court Reporter: Ron St. Claire CSR# 1170; Court Investigator: Shauna Gillespie-Ford; Bailiff: T. Aguerre; Clerk: C. Joy Guandique.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan (Counsel for Doris Butler, Personal Representative).

Mr. Cowan represented that he was prepared to file the Petition for Release of Funds from Blocked Account once the Court determined the specially-set hearing date for the same. With that representation, the Court set the matter for hearing on April 2, 2007 at 9:00 a.m in Dept. 204, annotating counsel's Notice of Hearing for the same. The present hearing was continued to that date as well.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Continued To Mar-12-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Cowan (Counsel for Doris Butler, Personal Representative), Doris Butler (Personal Representative, present in court).

Procedural Note: This matter was passed after the 10:30 a.m. recess as Ms. Butler had not yet returned after the recess and was thereafter called and heard at or about 10:35 a.m.

Mr. Cowan reported that the family had agreed to sell the real property but would need to seek the release of funds from a blocked account in order to secure a dumpster for removal of debris from the residence. The Court set a status date of March 12, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept 204 for filing of the Petition for release of funds. If the petition is filed prior to that date no appearance is necessary.

The hearing on the Order to show cause was continued to April 23, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for status on the sale of the real property.

Judicial Officer: Dorothy McMath, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katie Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Jimmy Peralta; Court Examiner: None; Court Clerk: R. Michael Diles.

MINI MINUTES: At 9:22am, No appearance made. Matter submitted.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

A 10:36am, Robert Cowan appearing for and with Doris Butler. Katherine Allen and Shirley Hodges are also in Court. Mr. Cowan reported to the Court that the parties have agreed to keep the house and not to sell it. They will fix up the property and rent it out for income. The property will be distributed as tenants in common. He is looking into missing assets from the conservatorship. He has the Substitution of Attorney ready to be filed today. Katherine Allen said they do not have any agreement to get a loan to fix up the property. Shirley Hodges stated her concerns on keeping the house. The Court wants all parties to meet and decide on what to do with the house. If there was an order approving the final account in the conservatorship it may be too late to object. Matter is continued to 2/26 for further status.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
